message:
fix(interactive): Enter accepts highlighted autocomplete item (issue #61)

The slash-command dropdown's footer reads "Enter/Tab accept", but Enter
was actually closing the dropdown and submitting the raw editor
contents — so a user who pressed Down to highlight `/model` and then
Enter would submit just `/` instead of accepting `/model`. That
mismatch is what issue #61 reported.

src/interactive.rs:
- In the autocomplete-open branch of `PiApp::update`, the `Enter`
  arm now checks `selected_item()` first. When the user has actively
  navigated to an item (selected is `Some(_)`), Enter calls
  `accept_autocomplete(&item)` and closes the popup — same path Tab
  takes.
- When nothing is highlighted (selected is `None`), Enter still
  closes the dropdown and falls through to the existing submit path.
  That preserves behavior for users who type `/foo` and hit Enter
  without ever pressing Down.
- The big comment block above the branch documents the new contract:
  Tab always accepts (auto-selects first item if needed), Enter
  accepts only when the user has actively navigated, otherwise
  submits raw. Listed conventions from fzf, vim completion, and
  Slack/IRC slash menus to anchor the choice.
- Tab arm is unchanged in behavior — the comment cruft about "If
  nothing is selected yet" is folded into the new top-of-branch
  block.

src/interactive/tests.rs:
- New `enter_accepts_highlighted_autocomplete_item` regression test:
  open the dropdown with one item `/model` and `selected = Some(0)`,
  send a `KeyType::Enter` message, assert input becomes `"/model "`
  (the item's `insert` payload) and the dropdown closes.
- New `enter_submits_when_no_autocomplete_item_highlighted` test
  asserts the dual contract: dropdown open, `selected = None`,
  Enter must still close the dropdown without forcibly accepting an
  arbitrary item.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
